title: senecio smithianus
intro: Senecio smithianus, also known as Smith's ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and emetic, although it is highly toxic and should be handled with caution. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of fever, due to its supposed antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Senecio smithianus is often used in small quantities, typically in combination with other plants, in remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
